meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use marketing automation to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by building a live, automated marketing engine. By using Claude Code to scaffold an intelligent content scheduler and Antigravity to deploy, you create a functional, high-value asset that demonstrates technical proficiency to employers while automating your own personal brand growth effortlessly.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen, the familiar weight of 'no experience' pinning your career aspirations to the floor. The industry demands a portfolio, yet the paradox remains: you need a job to get the experience, and experience to get the job. It is a cycle designed to filter out the uninitiated, but it is also a system waiting to be gamed by those who understand automation.

Today, we break that cycle. We are not just building another 'To-Do' list; we are engineering a marketing automation engine that runs 24/7. This isn't just code—it is proof of competence. By leveraging the synthesis of Antigravity and Claude Code, you will transform from a student with a blank GitHub into an architect of high-leverage software systems.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Architect the core workflow for your marketing tool. We focus on integrating a content API to fetch trending topics and auto-generate social media posts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust architecture for a marketing automation bot that fetches RSS feeds, summarizes content using an LLM, and prepares it for social media scheduling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure with TypeScript and Zod for schema validation'

steps.title: Build the Content Engine
steps.description: Implement the core logic that handles data ingestion and transformation. This demonstrates your ability to manage external API state and data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular service layer that handles API rate limiting and error handling for external social media platforms.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create content-service.ts with error handling and retry logic'

steps.title: Connect the LLM Layer
steps.description: Integrate Claude to provide intelligent, human-like summaries. This shows you can move beyond simple scripts into AI-driven application development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function using the Anthropic SDK to transform raw article text into engaging, platform-specific social media copy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate Anthropic SDK and define prompt templates for social media generation'

steps.title: Implement the Scheduler
steps.description: Set up a cron-job style scheduler to run your automation. This proves you understand persistent background tasks and server-side logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a cron job configuration to trigger the marketing engine at specific intervals using a lightweight background worker.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement node-cron scheduler with logging to track execution status'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Deploy the application to a cloud environment and set up logging. A live URL is worth a thousand lines of resume text.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an infrastructure-as-code configuration for deploying a containerized Node.js application to a cloud provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Dockerfile and deployment scripts for cloud deployment'

pro_tips: Always wrap your external API calls in a dedicated 'gateway' pattern to make swapping providers trivial in the future.
Use Zod for runtime type checking; it acts as documentation for your data structures and prevents silent failures.
Treat your logs as your primary debugging tool—if it isn't logged, it didn't happen in production.
Build a 'dry-run' mode into your automation so you can verify the output before it hits your social media channels.
Focus on the 'Why' in your README. Employers care less about the code and more about the business problem you solved.
content.next_steps: Once your bot is live, write a blog post detailing the challenges you solved, link it to your project, and add it to your LinkedIn profile to start attracting recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You now have a live, production-grade application that proves your worth to any hiring manager. Don't stop here; iterate on your tool, add analytics, and scale your reach. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
